note: seq=48 (QUEUE-THROUGHPUT TIMER, read-only monitoring). Installs a 5-min oneshot timer that runs the now-fast (5s sample) /opt/atlas/queue_throughput_probe.py and republishes status/hetzner/queue-throughput.json -- so the InterServer ceiling ramp can be watched live (combined done-rate, per-host claim split, shared pg_total_connections vs max_connections) without manual re-triggers. The probe is READ-ONLY (no writes to atlas tables). The service pulls STATUS_TOKEN/STATUS_REPO from /etc/atlas/autopull.env to publish. PG-light: a 5s sample every 5 min. NO stop/disable; daemon-reload/enable/start only. Re-fetches+sha-verifies the probe (pin = current fast version) so the timer never runs a stale copy.

steps.note: seq=48: 5-min read-only queue-throughput probe timer for live ramp monitoring.

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import hashlib,sys; w={'/opt/atlas/queue_throughput_probe.py':'7adb681d1c01de978d1a7a6fd7d56f825d07b9d23519019e7e27c79b0ddf59fd'}; [sys.exit('SHA MISMATCH '+p) for p,h in w.items() if hashlib.sha256(open(p,'rb').read()).hexdigest()!=h]; print('sha ok')"

steps.content:
[Unit]
Description=ATLAS shared-queue throughput probe (read-only; publishes status/hetzner/queue-throughput.json)
After=network-online.target postgresql.service
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=oneshot
Nice=15
IOSchedulingClass=idle
EnvironmentFile=-/etc/atlas/db.env
EnvironmentFile=-/etc/atlas/autopull.env
ExecStart=/opt/atlas/venv/bin/python /opt/atlas/queue_throughput_probe.py
TimeoutStartSec=60

[Unit]
Description=Run the ATLAS queue-throughput probe every 5 min

[Timer]
OnBootSec=2min
OnUnitActiveSec=5min
Persistent=true
Unit=atlas-qprobe.service

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target
